sections.title: Chief Complaint
sections.description: Primary presenting complaint in the patient's own words, with anatomical site and laterality (right/left) and duration when stated.

sections.title: History of Present Illness
sections.description: Onset, duration, mechanism (surgical, traumatic, pressure, vascular), and progression of the wound or concern; character of any pain; associated symptoms (drainage, odour, fever, increasing redness, dehiscence); prior wound care and dressings tried; relevant context (prior reconstruction, flap/graft, radiation, smoking status, nutrition) — each captured only as the patient or physician states it. Emit one claim per distinct element.

sections.title: Past Medical History
sections.description: Relevant active or past medical conditions affecting wound healing or perfusion as stated (e.g. diabetes, peripheral vascular disease, immunosuppression, prior radiation, smoking). Capture only what is mentioned; leave not_captured if none discussed.

sections.title: Medications
sections.description: Current medications with dose, route, and frequency exactly as stated (e.g. 'cefalexin 500 mg by mouth four times daily', 'apixaban 5 mg twice daily'); include anticoagulants, antibiotics, immunosuppressants, and relevant PRN analgesics. Leave not_captured if none discussed.

sections.title: Allergies
sections.description: Stated drug or material allergies (including adhesives, latex, topical agents) and the reaction when given. Leave not_captured if allergies are not addressed during the encounter.

sections.title: Wound Assessment
sections.description: Document each finding as the physician states it, leading with the structure: anatomical location and laterality; dimensions as length × width × depth in cm (and undermining/tunnelling location and depth in cm with clock position when given); wound-bed tissue types with proportions (e.g. '80% granulation, 20% slough', eschar, necrosis); exudate amount and character (scant/moderate/heavy; serous/serosanguineous/purulent; colour; odour); wound edges (approximated, rolled, undermined, tunnelling); periwound skin (intact, erythema, induration, maceration, callus); flap or graft viability (colour match, capillary refill in seconds, perfusion, warmth, turgor, any congestion or pallor); and signs of infection — each described only as stated, never inferring healing trajectory or aetiology. One claim per distinct finding.

sections.title: Imaging Review
sections.description: Imaging findings as literally described on screen — state modality, laterality, and the specific view/sequence for each finding. One claim per view or finding; findings described only, never interpreted beyond what the physician states. Leave not_captured if no imaging is reviewed.

sections.title: Assessment
sections.description: The physician's working assessment. When synthesis is permitted (grounded mode), present it as one claim per working assessment plus any contributing factors the physician relates, and ground each statement in the wound/exam/imaging/history source(s) it rests on (cite multiple sources where relevant). Never state an assessment the cited findings do not support; never add a differential or aetiology the physician did not voice.

sections.title: Plan
sections.description: The plan as stated by the physician, with one claim per item grouped by: investigations ordered, referrals, dressing/treatment options discussed with their trade-offs/cost when mentioned, risks counselled, activity and wound-care advice, and follow-up timing.
